function.name: check_calendar_availability
function.description: Query Ricardo's Microsoft 365 calendar for free/busy information within a specified date range. Returns only availability status (free/busy/tentative), never event titles or details. Read-only access.

start_date.description: Start of the date range to check (ISO 8601). Example: 2026-04-16T09:00:00Z

end_date.description: End of the date range to check (ISO 8601). Example: 2026-04-25T17:00:00Z

duration_minutes.description: Desired meeting duration in minutes. Used to filter slots that are long enough.

function.name: escalate_to_ricardo
function.description: Flag a message for Ricardo's personal attention. Sends a Telegram notification to Ricardo with inline approval buttons and holds any draft response for his review. Use this for student welfare matters, confidential content, high-importance decisions, emotional distress, or any query where you are not confident in the accuracy of your response.

reason.description: Category of escalation. Determines notification priority and routing.

summary.description: Brief (2-3 sentence) summary of the message content and why it requires Ricardo's attention.

urgency.description: How quickly Ricardo needs to see this. 'immediate' sends a push notification now. 'same_day' queues for the daily digest. 'on_return' adds to the return briefing.

draft_response.description: Your proposed response for Ricardo to review, edit, and approve before sending. Optional but strongly recommended.

original_message_id.description: The Message-ID header of the inbound email, for threading and reference.

function.name: send_email_reply
function.description: Send a response email via the Resend API. Ricardo is always included in CC automatically. The AI-generated footer is appended automatically by the orchestration layer; do not include it in the body.

body.description: The email body as plain text. Use line breaks for paragraph separation. Do not include the PAAIR footer; it is appended automatically.

in_reply_to.description: The Message-ID of the email being replied to, for correct threading.
